mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, which supports corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 65% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity and operational efficiency, which boosts short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to generate positive returns during recovery phases, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.02 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts reflects weakening real estate investment and reduced construction activity, which contracts economic output and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in 60% of cases following a 5% housing starts decline, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy with a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that increases wage income and consumer confidence, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 5-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values indicate accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in 70% of high-inflation periods, or the inflation-return correlation remains statistically insignificant with a p-value greater than 0.10 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that increases corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle phases, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance levels with a Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.
